paper_title: Insect Collection, Rearing, and Genetic Analysis

methods.description: Process for collecting insect specimens (Cricotopus festivellus and Cricotopus diversus) from various natural and non-natural habitats in the Netherlands, Norway, and the USA.

steps.description: Cricotopus festivellus material was collected in the Netherlands and Norway.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect insect specimens from specified locations in the Netherlands and Norway.
inputs: Collection locations in the Netherlands (non-natural stream, moorland pool, lake, ditch with seepage)
Collection locations in Norway (lakes, pond near Trondheim)

steps.description: Cricotopus diversus larvae were collected from Baismans Run, a small second-order stream in a forested catchment within Oregon Ridge Park, Baltimore County, Maryland (MD), USA.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect insect larvae from a specified location in the USA.
inputs: Collection location in the USA (Baismans Run, Maryland)

methods.description: Laboratory procedure for rearing collected Cricotopus diversus larvae under controlled conditions to obtain emerged adults and associated exuviae.

steps.description: Larvae of C. diversus were gently removed from rocks bearing attached algae and water moss.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate insect larvae from their natural substrate.
inputs: Collected Cricotopus diversus larvae on rocks/moss

steps.description: reared in individual aerated jars
steps.searchable_summary: Place larvae into individual aerated rearing containers.
outputs: Cricotopus diversus larvae in individual jars

steps.description: at 12:12 (L:D) photoperiod
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain a controlled 12:12 light:dark photoperiod for rearing.

steps.description: and temperature regime of 18C: 15C (D:N).
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain a controlled 18C day / 15C night temperature regime for rearing.

steps.description: Larvae were fed with epilithic algae from Baismans Run
steps.searchable_summary: Provide epilithic algae as food source to larvae.

steps.description: and water changes were conducted weekly.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform weekly water changes in rearing jars.

steps.description: Jars were inspected daily for emerged adults and associated pupal exuviae.
steps.searchable_summary: Inspect rearing jars daily to monitor for insect emergence.

methods.description: Laboratory procedure for preparing collected insect specimens (adults, exuviae) for subsequent morphological and genetic analysis.

steps.description: Two legs from each adult were used to obtain COI nucleotide sequence data
steps.searchable_summary: Sample tissue (legs) from adult specimens for genetic analysis.

steps.description: Specimens were prepared by clearing adults with proteinase K;
steps.searchable_summary: Chemically clear adult specimens using proteinase K.

steps.description: the adult exoskeleton was dissected
steps.searchable_summary: Dissect the exoskeleton from cleared adult specimens.

steps.description: and slidemounted in Euparal with the associated pupal and larval exuviae.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount dissected exoskeleton and associated exuviae onto microscope slides using Euparal.
outputs: Mounted specimens on slides (adult exoskeleton, pupal exuviae, larval exuviae)

methods.description: Process for obtaining COI gene sequence data for collected specimens, involving external sequencing services and accessing public databases.

steps.description: obtain COI nucleotide sequence data from the Canadian Centre for DNA Barcoding, following their standard procedures (Ratnasingham and Hebert 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Send tissue samples for COI sequencing by an external service following standard procedures.
inputs: External sequencing service (Canadian Centre for DNA Barcoding)

steps.description: we accessed the "Public Data Portal BIN Page" on BOLD for BOLD:AAP5924 (C. diversus) and BOLD:AAV1707 (C. festivellus).
steps.searchable_summary: Access specific Barcode Index Number (BIN) pages on the BOLD database.
outputs: BOLD BIN page information (specimen lists, metadata)

steps.description: FASTA files containing the COI sequences for each BIN were downloaded from BOLD.
steps.searchable_summary: Download COI sequence data files in FASTA format from BOLD.

methods.description: Computational analysis of COI sequence data using MEGA7 to calculate genetic distances within and between species.

steps.description: The MEGA7 package (Kumar et al. 2016) was used to compare genetic distances within and between species.
steps.searchable_summary: Utilize the MEGA7 software package for genetic distance analysis.

steps.description: Alignment of the combined sequences by nucleotide was carried out in MUSCLE;
steps.searchable_summary: Perform nucleotide sequence alignment using the MUSCLE algorithm.
inputs: FASTA files of COI sequences (from COI Sequence Data Acquisition)

steps.description: mean genetic distances within species and between C. diversus and C. festivellus were subsequently calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate mean genetic distances within species.

steps.description: mean genetic distances within species and between C. diversus and C. festivellus were subsequently calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate mean genetic distances between species (C. diversus and C. festivellus).
